segments.text: This is the word of the LORD about the nations—the word that came to Jeremiah the prophet

concerning Egypt and the army of Pharaoh Neco king of Egypt, which was defeated at Carchemish on the Euphrates River by Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon in the fourth year of Jehoiakim the son of Josiah king of Judah:
Deploy your shields, small and large; advance for battle!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Harness the horses; mount the steeds; take your positions with helmets on! Polish your spears; put on armor!
segments.speaker: Unknown
Unknown

segments.text: declares the LORD.
The swift cannot flee, and the warrior cannot escape! In the north by the River Euphratesthey stumble and fall.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Who is this, rising like the Nile, like rivers whose waters churn?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Egypt rises like the Nile, and its waters churn like rivers, boasting, ‘I will rise and cover the earth; I will destroy the cities and their people.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Advance, O horses! Race furiously, O chariots! Let the warriors come forth—Cush[^132] and Put carrying their shields, men of Lydia drawing the bow.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: For that day belongs to the Lord GOD of Hosts, a day of vengeance against His foes. The sword will devour until it is satisfied, until it is quenched with their blood. For the Lord GOD of Hosts will hold a sacrificein the land of the north by the River Euphrates.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Go up to Gilead for balm, O Virgin Daughter of Egypt! In vain you try many remedies, but for you there is no healing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The nations have heard of your shame, and your outcry fills the earth, because warrior stumbles over warrior and both of them have fallen together.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This is the word that the LORD spoke to Jeremiah the prophet about the coming of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon to strike the land of Egypt:
Announce it in Egypt, and proclaim it in Migdol; proclaim it in Memphis[^133] and Tahpanhes:‘Take your positions and prepare yourself, for the sword devours those around you.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Why have your warriors been laid low? They cannot st and, for the LORD has thrust them down.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
They continue to stumble; indeed, they have fallen over one another. They say, ‘Get up! Let us return to our people and to the land of our birth, 
away from the sword of the oppressor.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
There they will cry out:‘Pharaoh king of Egypt was all noise; 
he has let the appointed time pass him by.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: As surely as I live, declares the King, whose name is the LORD of Hosts, there will come one who is like Tabor among the mountains and like Carmel by the sea.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Pack your bags for exile, O daughter dwelling in Egypt! For Memphis will be laid waste, destroyed and uninhabited.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Egypt is a beautiful heifer, but a gadfly from the north is coming against her.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Even the mercenaries among herare like fattened calves. They too will turn back; together they will flee, they will not st and their ground, for the day of calamity is coming upon them—the time of their punishment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Egypt will hiss like a fleeing serpent, [^134] for the enemy will advance in force; with axes they will come against herlike woodsmen cutting down trees.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will chop down her forest, declares the LORD, dense though it may be, for they are more numerous than locusts; they cannot be counted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The Daughter of Egypt will be put to shame; she will be delivered into the hands of the people of the north.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, says:
Behold, I am about to punish Amon god of Thebes, [^135] along with Pharaoh, Egypt with her gods and kings, and those who trust in Pharaoh.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: I will deliver them into the hands of those who seek their lives—of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and his officers. But after this, Egypt will be inhabited as in days of old, declares the LORD.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: But you, O Jacob My servant, do not be afraid, and do not be dismayed, O Israel. For I will surely save you out of a distant place, your descendants from the land of their captivity! Jacob will return to quiet and ease, with no one to make him afraid.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts

segments.text: And you, My servant Jacob, do not be afraid, declares the LORD, for I am with you. Though I will completely destroy all the nations to which I have banished you, I will not completely destroy you. Yet I will discipline you justly, and will by no means leave you unpunished.
segments.speaker: the Lord of Hosts
